architecture.summary: Dreamer is a depth-recurrent Transformer that combines three orthogonal attention mechanisms: sequence attention (SA) over tokens, depth attention (DA) over previous depths of the same token, and expert attention (EA) as a sparse mixture-of-experts. The core single-layer architecture is repeated across depths, with parameter sharing and depth-position-encoded routing via RoPE. Information flows through the depth dimension via DA (addressing hidden-size bottleneck), through experts via sparse EA (addressing layer-size bottleneck), and through sequence via standard SA. DA uses a per-token KV cache that is overwritten after each token, keeping memory overhead constant. Attention mixtures are composed sequentially (DA → SA → EA) or partially parallel, with residuals normalized via RMSNorm applied to the stream itself.
trained_components: expert attention (EA) sparse routing and MLP experts
sparse MoE experts for attention projections (query/key/value and aggregation)
shared baseline expert in sparse attention MoEs

biblio.title: Depth-Recurrent Attention Mixtures: Giving Latent Reasoning the Attention it Deserves

claims.statement: Models require 2 to 8x fewer training tokens for the same accuracy as FLOP-, parameter-, and memory-matched SOTA
DR+DA with depth 16 outperforms LA with depth 32 on all reasoning benchmarks
Depth-recurrent models use 2 to 11x larger expert selection diversity than SOTA MoEs
Global expert balancing achieves Gini coefficient of ca. 0.075, indicating strong load balancing across depths
At depth 16: DR achieves 4.0x data efficiency; DR+DA achieves 2.9x data efficiency (average across benchmarks)
At depth 32: DR achieves 2.6x data efficiency; DR+DA achieves 3.5x data efficiency (average across benchmarks)

Depth attention enables deeper models to scale more effectively than shallow models; DA particularly alleviates hidden-size bottleneck which becomes more pronounced as depth increases and more reasoning shifts from sequence to depth dimension

claims.dataset: 1000 random validation sequences from DR+DA depth 32
claims.statement: Depth attention patterns show nontrivial routing: early depths look back to first few depths; middle depths are recalled later; final output informed by middle to high depths—extending expressivity beyond uniform skip-connections
